REQUIREMENTS Required of All Students Jewish Studies majors in all tracks take 15 credits of coursework consisting of 12 credits of foundations (usually taken in the ﬁrst two years of the major) and a capstone seminar (usually taken in the third or fourth year of the major). Up to two Hebrew or Yiddish language courses at any level can count toward the major (other languages with the written permission of JWST advisor).
